join_order.notes: adsh_code in [0, 86135) — build flat array sub_info[adsh_code]={cik,name_code,valid} (86135 slots × 12B ≈ 1MB). O(1) direct lookup during num scan. Faster than probing sub_adsh_hash.bin. Pre-built index not needed — direct array has zero hash cost.

filters.implementation: load num/uom_dict.txt at runtime via find_code(); compare int16_t uom[i] == usd_code. C2: never hardcode dict code.
check double value is finite (isfinite check or skip if all rows are non-null per dataset)

data_structures.sub_lookup: direct_flat_array[86135] of {int32_t cik, int32_t name_code}, sentinel cik=-1 for non-fy2022 entries. ~1MB total, fits L2. Built by scanning sub (86K rows), using std::fill sentinel init (C20: never memset for multi-byte sentinel).
data_structures.map_a_outer_groupby: thread_local open_addressing_hash_map — key=uint64_t packed((uint32_t)name_code << 32 | (uint32_t)cik), value=int64_t sum_cents. capacity=next_power_of_2(14600*2)=32768. slot=16B. per_thread_memory=512KB. 64_threads=32MB total. Bounded probing (C24). C9: capacity 2x estimated groups.
data_structures.map_b_having_groupby: thread_local open_addressing_hash_map — key=int32_t cik, value=int64_t sum_cents. capacity=32768. slot=16B. per_thread_memory=512KB. 64_threads=32MB total. Bounded probing (C24).
data_structures.aggregation_merge: two_phase_tree_merge: T×G = 64×14600 ≈ 930K (< 2M threshold). Merge groups of 8 threads in parallel (log₈(64)=2 rounds) then final 8→1. Reduces merge input 8× per round.
data_structures.topk: std::partial_sort or min-heap of size 100 on filtered Map A entries after HAVING (P6)

map_a.purpose: outer GROUP BY (name_code, cik) → int64_t sum_cents
map_a.value: int64_t sum_cents — C29 MANDATORY: accumulate as llround(value * 100.0), never double/float/long double/Kahan
map_a.note: C15: key MUST include both name_code AND cik. C29: int64_t cents only.

map_b.purpose: HAVING subquery GROUP BY cik → int64_t sum_cents

having_threshold.computation: grand_sum_cents = sum of all map_b values; num_ciks = count of distinct map_b entries; comparison: group_sum_cents * num_ciks > grand_sum_cents (integer multiply avoids division, exact)
having_threshold.note: HAVING threshold = AVG(per-cik sub_total). Avoid floating-point division by cross-multiplying.

parallel_phases: parallel_scan_num with thread-local Map A and Map B
two_phase_tree_merge for Map A and Map B
sequential_phases: compute_having_threshold (single pass over merged Map B)

indexes.purpose: NOT USED — superseded by direct flat array. Direct array [adsh_code] → {cik,name_code} gives O(1) with zero hash overhead and fits in L2. Pre-built index (2MB) would require hash probing; direct array is faster for this query.

format.name: decode int32_t name_code via sub/name_dict.txt → string
format.total_value: int64_t cents: bool neg=(cents<0); if(neg) cents=-cents; printf('%s%lld.%02lld', neg?'-':'', (long long)(cents/100), (long long)(cents%100))

critical_rules.C29: SUM(n.value) MUST accumulate as int64_t sum_cents += llround(value*100.0). Max observed value 5.94e13 >> 10^13 threshold. NEVER use double/float/long double/Kahan. Applies to both Map A and Map B accumulators.
critical_rules.C2: Load num/uom_dict.txt and sub/name_dict.txt at runtime. Use find_code() for USD lookup.
critical_rules.C9: Hash table capacity = next_power_of_2(estimated_groups * 2). Map A and Map B: next_power_of_2(14600*2)=32768.
critical_rules.C15: Map A key must be composite (name_code, cik) — both GROUP BY columns required.
critical_rules.C20: Use std::fill() to initialize sub_info array with sentinel, NOT memset().
critical_rules.C24: All hash table probe loops: bounded for-loop (probe < cap), not unbounded while.

memory_budget.hot_working_set: sub_flat_array(0.7MB) + per-thread maps(1MB) << L3 per core — fully cache resident
